title: browse roadmap v1.5–v2.2
overview: Full product roadmap from v1.5 through v2.2 — 6 versions spanning network intelligence, verification/CI, visual intelligence, native app automation, agent workflows, and an agent toolkit/SDK.
scopeChallenge: All 8 previous plans shipped. Current: ~23.5K src lines, 103 commands, v1.5.0. Ruled out: Lighthouse (perf-audit better), AI selectors (non-deterministic), visual regression service (screenshot-diff exists), browser cloud (partnered), full test framework (generates Playwright tests instead).
roadmapShape: v1.5-v1.7 deepen the existing browser CLI/MCP product with network inspection, assertions, export, visual diagnostics, accessibility checks, and lower-round-trip agent ergonomics.
v2.0 adds macOS app automation as a second runtime behind the same session/dispatch surface; browser behavior must remain unchanged.
v2.1 adds thin orchestration helpers (flow, retry, watch) over existing commands rather than a competing full test framework.
v2.2 adds SDK mode plus checked-in project files for detections, audit rules, saved flows, and config; npm plugins are explicitly out of scope.
antiDriftRules: Markdown and JSON are a paired artifact; task IDs, files, dependencies, priorities, acceptance criteria, and coverage entries must stay isomorphic.
Every acceptance criterion must be backed by at least one explicit test bullet in the owning test task or coverage map.
Any task that changes shared runtime behavior must own the shared wiring files instead of assuming they will be updated implicitly later.
Parser, formatter, generator, and diff logic require unit or contract tests in addition to integration tests.
Platform-specific features require mocked contract tests plus gated real-environment integration tests.
Packaging and distribution tasks require npm-pack-level verification of shipped artifacts, exports, and bundled binaries.

failureModes.risk: Response body capture slows page loads
failureModes.mitigation: Opt-in only. Skip binary. Cap per-entry body size and total session body bytes.

failureModes.risk: Memory from captured bodies still grows too high
failureModes.mitigation: Enforce separate captured-body byte budget; evict oldest body payloads first while preserving request metadata. Cover with explicit eviction tests.

failureModes.risk: Readiness signal reports settled:true too early on SPA transitions
failureModes.mitigation: Combine pending-network and DOM-mutation quiet-window signals, and cover delayed-mutation plus delayed-fetch fixtures before enabling settle mode.

failureModes.risk: Conditional flags hide real regressions by skipping too much
failureModes.mitigation: Guard flags must return explicit SKIP output and only suppress not-found, empty, or unchecked states, never malformed selectors or usage errors.

failureModes.risk: Settle mode hangs on long-polling pages
failureModes.mitigation: Add a timeout guard, exclude long-lived streaming or socket requests from readiness, and return an explicit timeout note instead of blocking indefinitely.

failureModes.risk: wait --request and expect --request drift into different matcher semantics
failureModes.mitigation: Reuse the same parser or helper for method, path, and status matching and cover success, timeout, and status-mismatch cases in one shared test suite.

failureModes.risk: expect polling burns CPU
failureModes.mitigation: 100ms interval. Use Playwright-native waits where possible and unit-test timeout and zero-timeout semantics.

failureModes.risk: Budget tests flap because live browser metrics vary
failureModes.mitigation: Extract deterministic budget evaluation logic and test it with fixed report fixtures instead of relying only on live perf runs.

failureModes.risk: Generated Playwright export is structurally present but syntactically broken
failureModes.mitigation: Require parse or compile validation of generated source in tests.

failureModes.risk: WCAG contrast wrong on gradients or images
failureModes.mitigation: Skip gradient or image backgrounds, document the limitation, and test the skip path explicitly.

failureModes.risk: macOS AX permission UX confusing
failureModes.mitigation: browse doctor checks permission and prints step-by-step remediation with System Settings guidance.

failureModes.risk: User denies or cannot find AX permission grant
failureModes.mitigation: browse --app fails fast with accessibility guidance. Mocked bridge tests and doctor output verify the recovery path.

failureModes.risk: Runtime split breaks existing browser sessions
failureModes.mitigation: Introduce shared session-target abstraction and require browser-regression coverage while app sessions are active.

failureModes.risk: Swift bridge packaging drifts from shipped npm artifact
failureModes.mitigation: Verify bridge asset presence through npm pack, not only local source builds.

failureModes.risk: AX tree too deep for Simulator
failureModes.mitigation: Depth limit 30. Filter in -i mode.

failureModes.risk: Flow YAML syntax confuses agents
failureModes.mitigation: Use explicit YAML parser dependency with line and column errors. Cover malformed YAML, duplicate keys, and unknown commands in parser tests.

failureModes.risk: Custom rules become a second plugin system via arbitrary code execution
failureModes.mitigation: Keep rule files declarative JSON only. Reject JS or eval-based rule shapes in schema validation and cover malformed or unknown rule types in contract tests.

failureModes.risk: Saved-flow serialization drifts from the v2.1 parser/runtime
failureModes.mitigation: Use one canonical YAML serializer and route flow run <name> through the same parser and executor path as file-based flows. Cover save-to-run round trips.

failureModes.risk: Startup flows cause hidden side effects or rerun on every command
failureModes.mitigation: Run startup flows only when a session is first created, print which flows ran, and support --no-startup-flows for the current invocation.

failureModes.risk: Project-local toolkit files are silently ignored or resolved from the wrong root
failureModes.mitigation: Resolve all toolkit paths from the detected project root, and warn or fail with file names and config keys when paths or schemas are invalid.

failureModes.risk: SDK mode ships but exports or tarball contents are wrong
failureModes.mitigation: Validate packed tarball import behavior in a temp install using the published exports surface.

testCoverageMap.codepath: a11y-audit findings (alt, label, heading, touch target, generic link, missing lang)
App mocked bridge failures (permission denied, stale ref, app missing)
Browser sessions remain stable while app sessions are active
Flow parser malformed YAML / duplicate keys / unknown commands
toolkit path resolution and invalid-path errors
startup flows run once + disable flag

tasks.title: Extend NetworkEntry with body/header fields
tasks.description: Add optional requestBody, requestHeaders, responseBody, responseHeaders to NetworkEntry in network/buffers.ts.

tasks.title: Capture request bodies in browser/manager.ts
tasks.description: Extend page.on('request') to capture postData() for POST/PUT/PATCH when body mode enabled.

tasks.title: Capture response bodies in browser/manager.ts
tasks.description: Extend page.on('response') to capture text bodies for json/text/xml/html/js/css. Binary as placeholder. Cap 256KB.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-session body-byte budget evicts oldest captured bodies while preserving request metadata

tasks.title: Add --network-bodies CLI flag and env var
tasks.description: Parse --network-bodies flag, send X-Browse-Network-Bodies header, support BROWSE_NETWORK_BODIES env and browse.json.
acceptanceCriteria: Flag parsed and sent as header
Server enables body capture on session

tasks.title: Add request read command
tasks.description: New read command: browse request <index|url-pattern> inspects a single network entry with full headers and bodies.
acceptanceCriteria: Clear error when bodies not captured

tasks.title: Add api meta command
tasks.description: New meta command: browse api <method> <url> runs fetch() in page context with cookies/auth. Returns status + headers + parsed body.

tasks.title: MCP tools + CLI help + chain for v1.5
tasks.description: Register request and api: MCP tools, CLI help, chain sets, SAFE_TO_RETRY for request.

tasks.title: Extend HAR export with bodies
tasks.description: Include request/response bodies in HAR 1.2 export when available.

tasks.title: Test fixtures and integration tests for v1.5
tasks.description: Add JSON API fixture routes plus a small SPA-style mutation fixture. Test network body capture, request/api, HAR with bodies, action-context readiness, guarded writes, selector suggestions, and settle mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: api GET with session cookie set via cookie command verifies cookie in request

tasks.title: Add page readiness signal to action-context
tasks.description: Extend action-context so write-command responses report whether the page is settled after the action, based on both pending network requests and recent DOM mutation activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Context-enabled write responses include settled:true or settled:false
Readiness combines networkPendingCount === 0 with a 300ms DOM-mutation quiet window
Unsettled output includes the blocking reason or reasons
DOM-mutation tracking resets on navigation and tab switch
Long-lived sockets or streaming requests do not keep the page permanently unsettled

tasks.title: Add guarded write flags and selector suggestions
tasks.description: Add conditional flags to common write commands and surface close-match suggestions on selector-not-found errors so agents can recover without full guard snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: click, hover, focus, and tap support if-exists and if-visible guards
fill supports if-empty and check supports if-unchecked
Skipped guarded actions return deterministic SKIP output instead of throwing
Unguarded selector-not-found errors include up to 3 concrete suggestions when close matches exist
Invalid selector syntax or malformed usage still throws immediately

tasks.title: Add session-scoped settle mode
tasks.description: Add an opt-in settle mode for sessions: browse set settle on or off. When enabled, write commands automatically wait for the page readiness signal before returning.
acceptanceCriteria: set settle on enables post-write settling for the active session and set settle off disables it
Navigation and interaction write commands wait for the readiness signal before returning
Default settle quiet window is 300ms with a documented timeout guard
wait and set settle do not recursively trigger settle mode
On settle timeout, the command returns its normal result plus an explicit unsettled timeout note instead of hanging indefinitely

tasks.title: Implement expect condition parser
tasks.description: Create src/expect.ts with parseExpectArgs and checkConditions for --url, --text, --visible, --hidden, --count, --request, --timeout.
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid comparator or malformed request-status combination throws clear usage error
Clear usage error on no conditions

tasks.title: Implement expect meta command
tasks.description: Add case 'expect' to meta.ts. Polls conditions in loop until pass or timeout. Clear error on failure.

tasks.title: Add request-matching mode to wait
tasks.description: Extend wait with --request <pattern> and optional --status N so agents can wait for a specific API request to complete before proceeding, reusing the same matcher semantics as expect --request.
acceptanceCriteria: wait --request pattern --status 200 returns when a matching completed request exists in the buffer or arrives before timeout
If status is omitted, the first completed matching request satisfies the wait
Request matcher semantics are shared with expect --request
Timeout error includes recent matching requests or last seen matching status
Status without request is a usage error
A matching request that started but did not finish before timeout does not satisfy the wait

tasks.title: Add --budget flag to perf-audit
tasks.description: Parse --budget lcp:2500,cls:0.1 flag. Compare metrics. Throw error on budget exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: Budget evaluation is deterministic and directly testable outside live browser timing

tasks.title: Extend exportReplay with expect steps
tasks.description: Serialize recorded expect commands as waitForElement/waitForExpression steps in Puppeteer Replay format.

tasks.title: Add exportPlaywrightTest function
tasks.description: New export function mapping recorded commands to Playwright Test API with expect() assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated Playwright source parses or compiles without syntax errors

tasks.title: MCP tools + CLI help for v1.6
tasks.description: Register expect MCP tool, update perf-audit description for --budget, add playwright export to help.

tasks.title: Integration tests for expect + wait-request + budget
tasks.description: Test all expect conditions, the request-matching wait mode, timeout behavior, request/status assertions, and deterministic budget evaluation.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser unit tests cover valid flag combinations and malformed comparator or request-status errors
expect --request + --status success and failure cases tested
wait --request timeout with clear last-seen-status output tested
Deterministic budget evaluator unit tests cover pass/fail and unmeasured metrics

tasks.title: Integration tests for export formats
tasks.description: Test replay with expects and Playwright test export.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated Playwright test parses as valid source

tasks.title: Visual evaluate — core infrastructure + helpers
tasks.description: Create src/visual.ts with landmark scan, parseColor, luminance, getEffectiveBg, overlaps, summarizeChildren.

tasks.title: Visual evaluate — anomaly detection
tasks.description: Add 5 anomaly detectors: contrast, overlap, overflow-x, overflow-hidden, viewport bleed.

tasks.title: Visual formatter + visual meta command
tasks.description: Format VisualReport as text. Register visual as META command.

tasks.title: Add layout read command
tasks.description: browse layout <selector> returns computed layout for element and positioning ancestors.

tasks.title: Implement a11y-audit meta command
tasks.description: WCAG 2.1 AA audit: contrast, missing alt, no label, heading skip, touch targets, generic links, missing lang. Score 0-100.

tasks.title: MCP tools + CLI help for v1.7
tasks.description: Register visual, layout, a11y-audit MCP tools. Update CLI help. Add to SAFE_TO_RETRY.

tasks.title: Test fixtures + integration tests for v1.7
tasks.description: Create visual-issues.html and a11y-issues.html fixtures. Test all detection types and visual helper functions.
acceptanceCriteria: a11y findings cover alt, label, heading, touch target, generic link, missing lang, and focus-order warning

tasks.title: Swift AX bridge — tree retrieval
tasks.description: Create browse-ax Swift CLI that reads AX tree of any macOS app by PID. Output JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: Each node: role, label, value, frame, children, actions

tasks.title: Swift AX bridge — action execution
tasks.description: Add action command: browse-ax --pid N action <path> <action>. Performs AXPress, AXConfirm, etc.

tasks.title: Swift AX bridge — set value + keyboard + screenshot
tasks.description: Add set-value, type, press, and screenshot commands to browse-ax.
acceptanceCriteria: type sends text input to focused element
press sends key event to focused element
Clear error when no focused element exists for type/press

tasks.title: Build system for Swift binary
tasks.description: Produce distributable macOS bridge artifacts, ship them in npm or lazy-download them, and resolve the correct binary at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Build pipeline produces arm64 and x86_64 artifacts and packages either a universal binary or explicit per-arch runtime selection
npm pack verification proves bridge artifact bootstrap files are present in the shipped tarball

tasks.title: AppManager — connect, snapshot, text
tasks.description: Create src/app-manager.ts. Spawns browse-ax, implements connect/snapshot (with @refs)/text.

tasks.title: AppManager — tap, fill, type, press
tasks.description: Add interaction methods to AppManager using browse-ax action/set-value/type/press commands.

tasks.title: App command dispatcher + --app flag
tasks.description: Create commands/app.ts, add --app flag to CLI, route app commands in server, and introduce a shared session-target abstraction so browser and app sessions share top-level dispatch safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared session-target abstraction preserves existing browser dispatch while enabling app sessions
Unsupported command gating happens centrally before execution

tasks.title: Action context for app commands
tasks.description: Wire before/after state capture into app commands. State = screen title + focused element.
acceptanceCriteria: Context line after tap shows screen change

tasks.title: MCP tools + CLI help for app commands
tasks.description: Register app MCP tools, update CLI help, and extend doctor to validate bridge availability plus Accessibility guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor prints explicit remediation steps when bridge or permission is missing

tasks.title: Integration tests for app automation
tasks.description: Test AppManager with mocked bridge-contract tests plus gated real macOS integration. Verify snapshot, tap, fill, context, unsupported-command handling, and browser/app session isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Mocked bridge failures cover permission denied, app missing, and stale ref
Browser session behavior remains stable while app session is active

tasks.title: Flow YAML parser
tasks.description: Create src/flow-parser.ts. Parses flow YAML into FlowStep[]. Validates commands and uses an explicit YAML parser dependency with line or column errors.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json adds explicit YAML parser dependency
Duplicate keys or unsupported step shapes fail with line or column context

tasks.title: Flow meta command
tasks.description: Add case 'flow' to meta.ts. Execute steps sequentially, stop on failure, report per-step.
acceptanceCriteria: Stops on failure with step number

tasks.title: Retry meta command
tasks.description: Wraps command in retry loop with --max attempts, --backoff, --until condition.

tasks.title: Watch meta command
tasks.description: Inject MutationObserver, poll for changes, execute callback command.

tasks.title: MCP tools + tests for v2.1
tasks.description: Register flow/retry/watch MCP tools. Write unit and integration tests for parser behavior and workflow failure paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Flow parser malformed-YAML, duplicate-key, and unknown-command tests exist
Retry max-failure path reports last error
Watch missing-selector and navigation-termination paths are tested

tasks.title: Custom audit rules
tasks.description: Add project-local audit rule loading from .browse/rules/*.json. Rules are declarative JSON artifacts checked into the project, not executable plugin code, and extend perf-audit plus a11y-audit with project-specific checks.
acceptanceCriteria: perf-audit and a11y-audit load .browse/rules/*.json alongside built-in rules
Rule format is versioned and declarative; arbitrary JS, eval, and plugin hooks are out of scope
Supports at least metric-threshold rules for perf-audit and selector-count rules for a11y-audit
Custom findings are labeled [custom] and include rule name plus source file path
Malformed JSON, unknown rule kind, or invalid target warns with file name and skips the file

tasks.title: Saved flows in .browse/flows/
tasks.description: Add project-local saved flows on top of the v2.1 workflow engine. flow save writes canonical YAML, flow run replays by name, and flow list enumerates available project flows.
acceptanceCriteria: flow save writes .browse/flows/<name>.yaml in deterministic canonical YAML
flow run <name> resolves from project-local flow directories and uses the same parser plus executor path as file-based flows
flow list prints saved flow names relative to the project root
Saved flows are normal project files with no install step
Saving without an active recording or path traversal attempts fail clearly

tasks.title: SDK mode — library entry point
tasks.description: Create src/sdk.ts that exports a clean typed API for direct programmatic browser use. SDK mode uses the same underlying browser/runtime primitives as browse commands but skips the HTTP server entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exports and files expose CLI plus SDK artifacts
npm pack temp-install verification proves createBrowser is importable from shipped tarball
CLI-only methods throw clear SDK-mode errors

tasks.title: Custom detection signatures
tasks.description: Extend the detection loader to scan .browse/detections/*.json for project-local signatures that teach browse about project-specific frameworks or stacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom JSON loaded alongside built-in signatures
Format is versioned and explicit with version: 1
[custom] label and source file path shown in output
Malformed JSON or invalid schema warns and skips the file

tasks.title: Agent Toolkit integration, MCP, and packaging tests
tasks.description: Write integration and contract tests for the full v2.2 toolkit surface: SDK mode, custom detections, custom audit rules, saved flows, config expansion, and the CLI plus MCP metadata that exposes them.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI help lists flow save, flow run, and flow list and documents the project-local toolkit surfaces
If flow is MCP-exposed from v2.1, the MCP tool schema and arg decoding cover save, run, and list actions without a second workflow tool
SDK createBrowser and packed tarball import tests pass
Custom detection and custom rule temp-project tests pass
flow save, flow run, and flow list temp-project tests pass
browse.json precedence and startup-flow behavior are tested
Malformed detection or rule JSON warns without crashing

tasks.title: Project config expansion
tasks.description: Expand browse.json into the project-local control plane for the agent toolkit. Config defines defaults and file roots for detections, rules, and flows while remaining subordinate to CLI flags and env vars.
acceptanceCriteria: browse.json supports defaultSession, defaultContext, startupFlows, detectionPaths, rulePaths, and flowPaths
Relative paths resolve from the detected project root
Precedence is CLI flags > env vars > browse.json > built-in defaults
startupFlows run only when a session is first created, not on every subsequent command
--no-startup-flows disables startup-flow execution for the current invocation
docs/agent-toolkit.md explains the .browse layout, config keys, precedence, and file-format expectations
Missing configured paths or unknown startup flow names fail with clear config-field errors
